- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

FlyManager.FEATURE_TRAIL_POOL_KEY_L : FlyManager.FEATURE_TRAIL_POOL_KEY_R;\\n        const prefab = useLeft ? this.m_featureTrailPrefabL : this.m_featureTrailPrefabR;\\n\\n        if ( !prefab )\\n        {\\n            log( `[FlyManager] PlayFeatureWheelTrailAnimation 失敗: 未設定 ${useLeft ? \\\"m_featureTrailPrefabL\\\" : \\\"m_featureTrailPrefabR\\\"}（colIndex=${colIndex}）` );\\n            callback?.();\\n            return;\\n        }\\n\\n        let effectNode: Node = null;\\n        try\\n        {\\n            effectNode = PrefabPoolManager.GetInstance().GetNode( poolKey, this.node );\\n            if ( !effectNode )\\n            {\\n                log( \\\"[FlyManager] PlayFeatureWheelTrailAnimation 失敗: 無法建立 Trail 節點\\\" );\\n                callback?.();\\n                return;\\n            }\\n\\n            const trailSpine = effectNode.getComponent( BaseSpine );\\n            if ( !trailSpine || !trailSpine.Spine )\\n            {\\n                log( \\\"[FlyManager] PlayFeatureWheelTrailAnimation 失敗: 無法取得 BaseSpine 元件\\\" );\\n                PrefabPoolManager.GetInstance().ReturnNode( poolKey, effectNode );\\n                callback?.();\\n                return;\\n            }\\n\\n            // Spine 產生於起點\\n            trailSpine.SetNodeActivity( true );\\n            effectNode.setWorldPosition( startWorldPosition );\\n\\n            // 左右 Spine 的 end bone 命名不同：左 \\\"Trail_End_Control\\\"、右 \\\"Trail_End_Control_R\\\"\\n            const endBoneName = useLeft ? 1;\\n\\n            trailSpine.PlayAnimation( animName );\\n\\n            const spineNode = trailSpine.Spine?.node ?? trailSpine.node;\\n            const endLocalPos = getNodeSpaceAR( spineNode, endWorldPosition );\\n            endBone.x = endLocalPos.x;\\n            endBone.y = endLocalPos.y;\\n\\n            // 回收時長對齊加速倍率（PlayAnimation 以 Game_Define.SpeedUp 設 timeScale）\\n            await Define.Wait( this, animDuration / Game_Define.SpeedUp );\\n\\n            trailSpine.SetNodeActivity( false );\\n            if ( effectNode?.isValid )\\n            {\\n                PrefabPoolManager.GetInstance().ReturnNode( poolKey, effectNode );\\n            }\\n            callback?.();\\n        }\\n        catch ( error )\\n        {\\n            log( \\\"[FlyManager] PlayFeatureWheelTrailAnimation error:\\\", error );\\n            if ( effectNode?.isValid )\\n            {\\n                PrefabPoolManager.GetInstance().ReturnNode( poolKey, effectNode );\\n            }\\n            callback?.();\\n        }\",\"numLines\":100,\"startLine\":740,\"totalLines\":1030}}"</outcome>

The user read the PlayFeatureWheelTrailAnimation function in detail. This shows the full implementation of the trail animation function. Key details:

1. The function sets the world position at the start (line ~794: effectNode.setWorldPosition(startWorldPosition))
2. It finds and manipulates the END bone positions (Trail_End_Control or Trail_End_Control_R)
3. But it does NOT manipulate the START bones (Trail_Start or Trail_Start_R) mentioned in the original request

The user wants to set the START bone positions, but the current code only sets:
- The node's world position (which positions the entire Spine)
- The END bone position to control where the trail goes

The problem is that the START bones (Trail_Start and Trail_Start_R) are not being explicitly positioned, which might be causing the incorrect starting point issue.

This is a discovery observation - understanding how the current implementation works and identifying what's missing.

  </facts>
  <narrative>PlayFlyToCollectAnimation provides the reference implementation for proper bone manipulation. It dynamically selects bone names based on animation type (Long/Short) and direction (_L/_R suffix), validates both bones exist, and crucially uses the Spine skeleton node rather than the outer wrapper node for coordinate conversion to avoid position drift when parent nodes have scale transformations applied. This pattern should be replicated in PlayFeatureWheelTrailAnimation to properly set Trail_Start and Trail_Start_R bone positions.</narrative>

    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
  </concepts>

const controller = effectNode.getComponent( FlyToCollectController );\\n           \\n\\n            // 播放動畫並套用骨頭位置（參照 PlayCollectTrailEffect）\\n            const animName = isLong ? endBone.y = endLocalPos.y;\\n\\n            controller?.PlayCollectAnimation( endWorldPosition );\\n\\n            await Define.Wait( this, animDuration - 0.4);\\n            callback?.();\\n\\n intervalSeconds 每個 spine 之間間隔幾秒\\n     * @param callback 可選：全部演出結束後回調\\n     */\\n    private async PlayAddCashAnimation(\\n        countPerRound: number,\\n        columnsToDrop: number[],\\n 播放投入 Cash 音效\\n        const addCashAudioId = AudioManager.Play(AudioManager.AudioClips.AddCash);\\n\\n        for ( let i = 0; i < playList.length; i++ )\\n        {\\n            const col = This is a significant edit where the user has modified the PlayFeatureWheelTrailAnimation function to add the start bone retrieval and validation. The changes:

1. Added startBoneName constant that selects "Trail_Start" for left or "Trail_Start_R" for right
2. Added startBone retrieval using findBone
3. Updated validation to check both startBone and endBone (changed from `if (!endBone)` to `if (!startBone || !endBone)`)
4. Updated the error log message to include both bone names

This follows the pattern established by other animation functions in the file. However, I notice they haven't yet SET the start bone's x and y coordinates - they've only retrieved and validated it. The actual position setting is still missing.

This is an incremental fix - they're halfway there. They've identified the bones but haven't applied the coordinates yet.

  <subtitle>PlayFeatureWheelTrailAnimation now finds and validates both start and end bones before animation playback</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Added startBoneName selection logic at line 794: Trail_Start for left trails, Trail_Start_R for right trails</fact>
    <fact>Added startBone retrieval using trailSpine.Spine.findBone at line 796</fact>
    <fact>Updated bone validation to check both startBone and endBone existence at line 797</fact>
    <fact>Updated error log message to report both missing bone names for debugging</fact>
    <fact>Start bone position coordinates are not yet set, only retrieved and validated</fact>
  </facts>

However, the fix is incomplete: while the bones are now found and validated, their x and y coordinates are not yet set using getNodeSpaceAR to convert startWorldPosition to local space, which is the actual position manipulation needed to correct the visual starting point.</narrative>

The user has now completed the fix for the trail animation starting point issue. Let me analyze what was done:

1. In the previous edit, they added:
   - `startBoneName` selection logic (Trail_Start for left, Trail_Start_R for right)
   - `startBone` retrieval using findBone
   - Validation for both startBone and endBone

2. In this latest edit, they added:
   - `startLocalPos` calculation using `getNodeSpaceAR(spineNode, startWorldPosition)` to convert world position to local space
   - Setting the start bone's x and y coordinates: `startBone.x = startLocalPos.x; startBone.y = startLocalPos.y;`

This completes the fix. The function now:
- Retrieves both start and end bones
- Converts both start and end world positions to local coordinates
- Sets both start and end bone positions

This matches the pattern used in PlayFlyToCollectAnimation and PlayShowScatterToMagicPotAnimation, which was the correct approach. The trail animation should now have the correct starting point because the Trail_Start/Trail_Start_R bones are being explicitly positioned at the startWorldPosition.

This is a complete bugfix - the issue has been resolved.

<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Fixed trail animation incorrect starting point by setting Trail_Start bone positions</title>
  <subtitle>Complete fix adds startLocalPos conversion and sets startBone x/y coordinates matching established pattern</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Added startLocalPos calculation at line 817 using getNodeSpaceAR to convert startWorldPosition to Spine node local space</fact>
    <fact>Set startBone.x and startBone.y coordinates at lines 818-819 to position the trail start point correctly</fact>
    <fact>Fix follows the same pattern as PlayFlyToCollectAnimation and PlayShowScatterToMagicPotAnimation which both set start and end bone positions</fact>
    <fact>Bone coordinates use spineNode reference instead of outer wrapper node to avoid parent scale transformation issues</fact>
    <fact>PlayFeatureWheelTrailAnimation now properly controls both Trail_Start/Trail_Start_R and Trail_End_Control bones for complete trail positioning</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>User completed the fix for incorrect trail animation starting points by adding the missing startLocalPos coordinate conversion and setting the start bone positions. The function now converts startWorldPosition to the Spine skeleton node's local coordinate space using getNodeSpaceAR, then explicitly sets the Trail_Start (left) or Trail_Start_R (right) bone's x and y coordinates. This matches the established pattern used by other animation functions in FlyManager and ensures the trail visual starting point is controlled programmatically rather than relying on default bone positions in the Spine animation data. The fix addresses the user's original concern that the trail animation was starting from the wrong position.</narrative>
